non_blocking_risks.risk: If shared-context ownership changes after execution-supervision.json is produced, executor-interface.json and writeback-ledger expectations must be updated together.

profile_adherence.evidence: The run stayed autonomous and deep, but kept probing bounded to source-aligned orchestration core.

same_source_consistency.evidence: The reusable template chain, shared-context plan, orchestration topology, and writeback evidence all refer to the same bounded workflow object.

cross_format_consistency.evidence: The execution packets and writeback targets preserve the same template handoff semantics already defined upstream.

fact_validation_chain.item: Confirm template-chain.json defines stable templates, variable mappings, and handoff rules.
Confirm shared-context-map.json defines shared files, ownership, read scope, and write scope.
Confirm orchestration-plan.json and execution-supervision.json consume those shared-context assumptions rather than redefining them.
Confirm executor-interface.json expresses execution_units and reference_context_refs rather than relying on informal child-agent identity.
If pruning or replacement is active, confirm memory-pruning.json and writeback-ledger.json align on memory_edit_events and replacement summaries.
Confirm writeback-ledger.json is present because execution-side shared-file coordination is active.

unverifiable_assumptions.item: The current executor-capability-registry.json remains valid for the target execution environment and will not require a later discovery step.

check.note: template-chain.json exists and defines reusable method handoffs for downstream execution design.
shared-context-map.json, orchestration-plan.json, execution-supervision.json, executor-interface.json, executor-capability-registry.json, and writeback-ledger.json exist.

scope_alignment_checks.item: The round stays inside source-aligned orchestration core and does not activate runtime/provider/governance operations extension.

check.note: shared-context-map.json and other orchestration core artifacts were generated before writeback audit and finish-oriented packaging completed.
writeback-ledger.json exists because shared-file coordination and checkpoint writebacks are active.

dispatch_recommendations.item: Proceed because template semantics remain upstream, execution topology remains downstream, and writeback evidence is preserved explicitly.

check.note: The autonomous deep profile justified a bounded orchestration-core probe without drifting into runtime/provider/governance operations.

check.why: Without it, execution packets would not have a stable reusable method chain to consume.
check.evidence: mode-resolution.json explicitly hands off template-chain.json into orchestration artifacts.

check.why: It bridges reusable upstream handoff semantics and execution-side coordination policy.
check.evidence: execution-supervision.json and executor-interface.json both depend on its ownership and scope assumptions.

check.why: Without it, execution identity and reference-context bindings may collapse back into informal agent wording.
check.evidence: execution_units, reference_context_refs, and required_capability_bindings are the explicit replacement surface for child-agent semantics.

profile_adjustment_recommendation.adjustment_reason: The profile was appropriately proactive for template compression plus orchestration-core handoff, with no need to switch cadence or probe strength.

evaluation_report.score_reason: The example cleanly demonstrates template-to-orchestration handoff, but still covers only one execution-heavy interaction pattern.
